actions.title: Follow up on Churchill advisor-coach onboarding
actions.context:
**Background**: Cian had an onboarding discussion with Jane from Churchill Leadership about joining their advisor-coach roster as a contractor. Churchill works with corporate and government clients providing executive coaching, team coaching, and organizational development services.

**Discussion**: Jane explained the full engagement model:
- Contractors receive 60% of what Churchill charges clients
- Corporate executive coaching engagements range from $16K-$25K for six months
- Advisor-coach pricing will be higher than standard coaching (still being figured out)
- Typical commitment is 2-3 hours/month of coaching plus alignment meetings
- Churchill retains client relationships; any client introduced through Churchill remains a Churchill client
- Coaches can bring opportunities to Churchill for larger engagements and receive a piece of all revenue
- Cian's sweet spot: technology strategy, translating between management and technology teams, turning strategy into execution
- Notable clients for credibility: NFL, Bell MTS, BC Housing (government AI consulting), Trillium

**Decision/Direction**: Jane said Churchill is updating their system to handle advisor-coaches separately from executive coaches, including a second bio format. She mentioned an internal meeting on Monday to finalize this. Jane committed to coming back in **approximately two weeks** (target: ~2026-04-30) to introduce Cian to **Lisa, their country director**, who will handle the formal onboarding process.

**Next steps**: Wait for Jane's follow-up in ~2 weeks. When contacted, Cian will need to: (1) review and sign the Master Services Agreement, (2) fill in an onboarding form, (3) complete a bio template. Cian noted the MSA should be with his Canadian company initially, with the understanding it can be redone later when he moves to a Florida LLC within the next year. Background check and insurance are only required when an engagement starts.

actions.title: Prepare Churchill advisor-coach bio highlighting Zen and tech background
actions.context:
**Background**: As part of the Churchill onboarding process, Cian will need to complete a bio template. Churchill is creating a separate bio format specifically for advisor-coaches (distinct from their standard executive coaching bios).

**Discussion**: Jane emphasized that the bio should highlight Cian's unique combination of skills. She specifically noted that including both the technology strategy background AND the contemplative/Zen Buddhist priest background would be a strong differentiator for the right clients. Jane mentioned they like to include a personal "about the coach" section — she gave the example of a coach whose marathon running helped a leader choose her. Jane said: *"Including the whole technology background but then the contemplative and zen background is really a value add for the right client."*

Cian's key credentials to feature: 25 years translating between executives and technology teams, NFL consulting, BC Housing AI implementation, Bell MTS, government compliance/privacy expertise, Zen Buddhist priest who integrates contemplative practices into coaching.

**Decision/Direction**: Both agreed the Zen/tech combination is compelling. Churchill will provide a bio template; Cian should prepare content highlighting both dimensions.

**Next steps**: When Lisa reaches out with the bio template, have content ready covering: technology strategy expertise, notable clients (NFL, government), and the Zen Buddhist priest background as a differentiator.
